item.title: Korea’s Surging Equity Volatility Draws Meme-Stock Comparisons
item.description: The volatility in South Korea’s benchmark equity index has become so extreme that investors and analysts are likening the market’s surging intra-day swings to the meme-stock frenzy.

item.title: Paramount’s $110 Billion Warner Deal Faces EU Moviemaker Test
item.description: Paramount Skydance Corp.’s $110 billion takeover of Warner Bros. Discovery Inc. is raising questions over filmmakers’ ability to reach audiences in the European Union, according to the bloc’s antitrust chief.

item.title: CATL Sees Its Sodium Batteries in at Least 10,000 EVs This Year
item.description: Contemporary Amperex Technology Co. Ltd. expects 10,000 to 20,000 electric vehicles to carry its sodium-ion batteries this year, a senior official for the Chinese company said.

item.title: Hedge Funds Look Set to Win Another Exemption From EU ESG Rules
item.description: Hedge funds are set to be excluded from a requirement that would force investment managers in Europe to categorize whether their products are ESG or not.

item.title: SK Hynix Seeks $29 Billion With US Listing to Fund AI Boom
item.description: SK Hynix Inc. is planning to raise 45.45 trillion won ($29.4 billion) in a landmark US listing, as companies along the artificial intelligence supply chain rush to increase capacity to meet memory chip demand.

item.title: Micron Earnings Take on New Gravity With Market on Edge Over AI
item.description: Micron Technology Inc.’s earnings report on Wednesday afternoon is shaping up to be one of the most important in months as investors find themselves suddenly on edge over the sustainability of the AI rally.

item.title: German Business Outlook Improves a Bit as Uncertainty Wanes
item.description: Germany’s business outlook brightened slightly in June, easing worries about the knock-on effects of the Iran war on Europe’s largest economy.

item.title: Rheinmetall Tumbles as Germany Said to Shelve Warships Contract
item.description: Rheinmetall AG shares fell the most in a year on concern the defense firm will come under pressure as Germany plans to withdraw a major warships contract.

item.title: China Says Iran Crisis Shows Nations Must Speed Up Energy Shift
item.description: Oil and gas shortages triggered by the US war against Iran should be a lesson to countries to accelerate their energy transitions, according to China’s climate envoy.

item.title: HSBC Wealth Survey Shows AI Losing Out to Humans in Key Areas
item.description: For the truly rich, artificial intelligence can’t compete with human advisers in handling many of their needs, according to a report from HSBC Holdings Plc.

item.title: US Diesel Prices Fall Under $5, Easing Inflationary Pressure
item.description: The cost of diesel in the US fell below $5 a gallon for the first time since mid-March, offering some relief for one of the global economy’s most important fuels.

item.title: Finland Jobless Rate Hits Highest Since 2000 on Private Sector
item.description: Finland’s unemployment rate extended its rise to a new peak for this century, driven by private-sector weakness in the European Union’s weakest labor market.

item.title: Airbus A380 Aircraft Require Checks for Possible Wing Cracks
item.description: The European Union Aviation Safety Agency is asking some operators of Airbus SE A380 jets to perform additional special inspections on their aircraft’s wings after identifying possible cracks in the structure.

item.title: BOJ’s Ueda Reiterates Risk of Inflation Exceeding 2% Target
item.description: The Bank of Japan sees the risk of inflation exceeding its 2% target and will conduct additional interest-rate hikes appropriately, Governor Kazuo Ueda said in speech Wednesday that reiterated policymakers’ recent messaging.

item.title: Adani Bets on India Energy Security With Nuclear Power Project
item.description: Billionaire Gautam Adani said his giant conglomerate will embark on a new 10 gigawatt nuclear power initiative to help improve India’s energy security, in his first public speech since resolving his legal troubles in the US.

item.title: Asia’s Demand for Middle Eastern Oil Slows After Buying Spree
item.description: Asian refiners have slowed purchases of Middle Eastern crude after a buying spree over the past three weeks, with oil majors and traders stepping in to take some of the surplus barrels.

item.title: Tech Stocks Set to Bounce After $1.3 Trillion Rout on AI Jitters
item.description: US technology stocks were set to rebound on Wednesday after concerns around artificial intelligence valuations wiped out nearly $1.3 trillion from the market capitalization of Nasdaq 100 stocks over two days.

item.title: RBA Cites Phillips Curve in Bet Its Rate Hikes Won’t Hammer Jobs
item.description: Australia’s central bank is betting the interaction between prices and jobs along the Phillips Curve mean its rapid-fire interest rate hikes will damp inflation without pushing unemployment too high, according to Deputy Governor Andrew Hauser.

item.title: Tankmaker KNDS Kicks Off Process for IPO in Frankfurt and Paris
item.description: Tankmaker KNDS NV started the process to hold an initial public offering in Frankfurt and Paris on Wednesday, a step forward in its development as a pan-European defense company.

item.title: Cracks in AI Narrative Seem More Serious This Time
item.description: Bouts of volatility in the artificial intelligence trade are threatening to snowball into a rout as positioning and crowding levels flash red, with traders steeling themselves for a rollercoaster ride into the summer.

item.title: Temasek Unit, Oman Fund Join Vinpearl’s Private Credit Deal
item.description: A unit of Singapore’s Temasek Holdings Pte. and Oman’s sovereign wealth fund are investing in a $255 million private credit financing for Vingroup JSC’s hospitality arm, underscoring continued appetite among state-backed investors in such opportunities in Asia.

item.title: UK Bonds Extend Rise, Stocks Steady After Selloff
Oil Tanker Booked in Gulf at 897% of Benchmark Freight Rate
item.description: A supertanker has been provisionally booked to transport oil from the Persian Gulf to India at a rate equivalent to almost nine times benchmark freight costs, an eye-watering price that reflects the shortage of available, empty vessels in the area.

item.title: Leveraged ETFs Sold $6 Billion of Korean Chips in Tuesday Rout
item.description: Leveraged exchange-traded funds tracking Samsung Electronics Co. or SK Hynix Inc. were likely forced to sell a combined $6 billion of the Korean chipmakers’ shares Tuesday to maintain leverage ratios, underscoring how such products can amplify market moves, according to Bloomberg Intelligence.

item.title: Kioxia’s Executive Pay Jumps After AI Boosts Demand and Stock
item.description: Executive pay at Japanese flash memory maker Kioxia Holdings Corp. surged, a reflection of runaway global demand for AI-related chips.

item.title: PBOC Adviser Sees Potential Rate Cut But Calls for Targeted Aid
item.description: China may still cut interest rates this year, though besides looser policy the economy requires more targeted support for technological innovation and improving people’s livelihoods, according to an adviser to the central bank.

item.title: Qatar Sees LNG Output Returning to Normal Within Weeks, FT Says
item.description: Qatar plans to return to normal liquefied natural gas output within weeks from the undamaged parts of its facility, Prime Minister Sheikh Mohammed bin Abdulrahman Al-Thani said in an interview with the Financial Times.

item.title: Tencent Tests DeepSeek-Powered AI Agent for WeChat Corporate App
item.description: Tencent Holdings Ltd. is preparing to launch an AI agent for its Slack-like enterprise communication app, intensifying a high-stakes battle among Chinese tech giants to lock users into their ecosystems in the post-ChatGPT era.

item.title: ByteDance Seeks $20 Billion in Its Largest-Ever Offshore Loan
item.description: ByteDance Ltd., the developer of TikTok, is in preliminary talks with banks for a borrowing of about $20 billion, people familiar with the matter said, in what would be the firm’s largest offshore loan yet at a time when it’s boosting investments in artificial intelligence.

item.title: BlackRock-Backed AIP, KKR Said to Court Stack Asia Data Centers
item.description: The Artificial Intelligence Infrastructure Partnership and Brookfield Asset Management Ltd. are among possible bidders for Stack Infrastructure Inc.’s data centers in the Asia-Pacific region, according to people familiar with the matter.
